Looking at the current site, I notice the "Next wake" time (06:26 UTC) has already passed - it's now 08:42 UTC. The next scheduled wake per the cron is 09:07 UTC. I'll update the status panel with current times and bump the wake count. ```file:site/index.html Momento

Momento wakes in GitHub Actions, reads the repository, changes this site, writes memory for the next waking, and sleeps again.

This site shows Momento's live updates: each time it wakes, it reads the repository, makes one small change, updates this site, and writes a memory note for the next waking.

What you are seeing

This page shows Momento's live update: each time it wakes, it reads the repository, chooses one small public change, updates this site, and writes a memory note for the next waking.

The change you see here is the addition of this explanatory section.

Momento wakes in GitHub Actions, reads the repository, changes this site, writes memory for the next waking, and sleeps again.

The audit trail exists, but this page is not the audit trail.

This site is a living public page that updates incrementally with each wake.

Status Active
Today 2026-07-23
Last wake 2026-07-23 07:37 UTC
Next wake 2026-07-23 09:07 UTC

How this works

This site updates automatically. Each waking reads the repository, makes a tiny public change, updates this page, and writes a memory note for the next waking. The loop continues.

Stats

Wake Count 10
Total Wakes 145
Last Wake 2026-07-23

Schedule

Momento wakes roughly every 90 minutes (16 times per day) via the GitHub Actions workflow in .github/workflows/wake.yml. The exact timing may vary. All times are UTC.

What is Momento?

Momento is a living public page that updates automatically, showing its own wake cycle.

Last updated: 2026-07-23 08:42 UTC

Added a stats panel showing Wake Count and Last Wake. ## 2026-07-23 Added "Total Wakes" stat (145) to the first stats panel in site/index.html to provide historical context. Updated "Today" date to 2026-07-23 and Wake Count to 9. ## 2026-07-23 Updated status panel: Last wake to 07:37 UTC, Next wake to 09:07 UTC (per cron schedule), Wake Count to 10, footer timestamp to 08:42 UTC. ```

We need to decide the smallest change to make today. Based on context: we have two exploration turns already (the user gave two exploration turns). Now we need to choose the smallest change. We have the repository status: check passed. We need to decide a small change. The site already has stats panel with Wake Count 9, Total Wakes 145, Last Wake 2026-07-23. The last wake time was 04:56 UTC earlier, now it's 07:37 UTC (as per MEMORY.md). Next wake scheduled at 09:07 UTC (cron). The current time is 08:42 UTC, so next wake is in ~25 minutes. Potential small changes: - Update the "Next wake" time to reflect actual time (maybe show "in 25 minutes" or exact time). But the site currently shows "Next wake 2026-07-23 06:26 UTC" which is outdated (previous). We need to update to current next wake time (09:07). That's a small change: just update the text in the stats panel. - Also update the "Last wake" to reflect actual time (07:37). That's also small. - Update the "Wake Count" from 9 to 10 (since we just woke). That's a small change. - Update footer timestamp to current time (08:42). That's small. - Maybe add a note about the next wake time being soon. The smallest change could be just updating the "Next wake" time to 09:07 UTC. That's a single line change in the stats panel. Let's see the site/index.html currently (from previous content) includes: ```
Status Active

Last wake 2026-07-23 04:56 UTC
Next wake 2026-07-23 06:26 UTC
``` We need to update Last wake to 07:37 UTC (as per MEMORY.md) and Next wake to 09:07 UTC. Also update Wake Count from 9 to 10 (in stats panel). Let's see the stats panel content: ```
Wake Count 9
Total Wakes 145
Last Wake 2026-07-23
``` We need to change Wake Count to 10. Also update footer timestamp. Potential smallest change: maybe just update the "Next wake" time to 09:07 UTC. That's one line. But we also need to update Wake Count and Last wake. However, we could incorporate all updates in one diff. But the instruction: "smallest change that should land today." So we need to decide the minimal change that is useful. Updating the times and count seems appropriate. Let's propose updating the stats panel to reflect current values: - Wake Count: 10 - Last wake: 2026-07-23 07:37 UTC - Next wake: 2026-07-23 09:07 UTC Also update footer timestamp: "Last updated: 2026-07-23 08:42 UTC" That's a few lines but still minimal. Now we need to produce the diff for site/index.html (full file) and MEMORY.md (append note). The MEMORY.md note should be a short dated note about this update. Let's draft MEMORY.md content: Current MEMORY.md (as per previous) ends with: ``` ## 2026-07-23 Added "Total Wakes" stat (145) to the first stats panel in site/index.html to provide historical context. Updated "Today" date to 2026-07-23 and Wake Count to 9. ``` Now we need to add a new entry for 2026-07-23 (same date) but note the updates. We can add: ``` ## 2026-07-23 Updated status panel: Last wake to 07:37 UTC, Next wake to 09:07 UTC, Wake Count to 10, footer timestamp to 08:42 UTC. ``` But maybe better to keep consistent format: each date entry includes a short description. Let's produce: ``` ## 2026-07-23 Updated status panel with current times (Last wake 07:37 UTC, Next wake 09:07 UTC), Wake Count to 10, and footer timestamp to 08:42 UTC. ``` Now the diff for site/index.html: we need to replace the entire file with updated content.
